name: Muro Novo - circular from Abrela Beach
name_original: Muro Novo – circular desde la Playa de Abrela
region_intro: Hiking around O Vicedo offers diverse landscapes along the Lugo coast of Galicia. The region features a rugged coastline with impressive cliffs and numerous beaches, including San Román and Abrela. It is characterized by its dramatic rock formations, estuaries like the Ria do Barqueiro, and a protected natural area designated for its significant coastal value. These natural features provide varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around O Vicedo The most popular hiking route is Punta de Fuciño do Porco - circular from San Román beach - Camino Natural de la Ruta del Cantábrico, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 17 minutes to complete. This moderate route features wooden walkways along cliffs with views of the Cantabrian Sea. Another top favourite among local hikers is Muro Novo - circular from Abrela Beach, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path. This challenging route explores the natural environment around Abrela Beach, requiring good fitness. Local hikers also love the Fuciño do Porco Point – Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint loop from Mosende, a 2.4 miles (3.8 km) trail leading through coastal scenery to the Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Hiking around O Vicedo is defined by coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and picturesque beaches. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more demanding treks. The routes in O Vicedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 1200 hikers have used komoot to explore O Vicedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in O Vicedo?
A: O Vicedo offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 10 easy, 18 moderate, and 4 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in O Vicedo?
A: Hiking in O Vicedo primarily features coastal paths, dramatic cliffs, and picturesque beaches. You'll encounter well-maintained paths and forest tracks, with some routes incorporating unique wooden walkways that hug the coastline, particularly around Fuciño do Porco. The terrain varies from gentle gradients to more challenging ascents on demanding routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in O Vicedo?
A: Yes, O Vicedo has several easy loops perfect for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5364409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point – Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint loop from Mosende&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8 km trail that takes about an hour, offering coastal scenery without significant difficulty. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10606799" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Román Beach – Fuciño do Porco Viewpoint loop from Mosende&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.08 km and also considered easy.

Q: What are the best trails for experiencing O Vicedo's coastal views?
A: For breathtaking coastal views, the trails around Fuciño do Porco are highly recommended. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975212671/punta-de-fucino-do-porco-circular-from-san-roman-beach-camino-natural-de-la-ruta-del-cantabrico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Fuciño do Porco - circular from San Román beach&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route featuring impressive wooden walkways over the sea. The PR-G 156 Costa do Vicedo also offers stunning views as it extends along the Lugo coast from the Ria of Barqueiro to San Román Beach.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in O Vicedo?
A: Yes, O Vicedo offers several circular routes. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975212671/punta-de-fucino-do-porco-circular-from-san-roman-beach-camino-natural-de-la-ruta-del-cantabrico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Fuciño do Porco - circular from San Román beach&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking a more challenging loop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979355870/muro-novo-circular-from-abrela-beach" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muro Novo - circular from Abrela Beach&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 17.1 km path that explores the natural environment around Abrela Beach.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the trails?
A: O Vicedo's trails lead to several notable natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;, known for its unique shape and wooden walkways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3175098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Estaca de Bares&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tixoso Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offer spectacular panoramic views of the coastline and the Cantabrian Sea. Additionally, the Illa Coelleira Viewpoint provides views of the island, an important ornithological site.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in O Vicedo?
A: The hiking experience in O Vicedo is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning coastal scenery, the dramatic cliffs, and the beautiful beaches encountered along the routes. The variety of trails, from easy strolls to challenging treks, is also a highlight for many visitors.

Q: Are there any historical or man-made attractions near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can find some interesting man-made attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/426289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estaca de Bares Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent landmark. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V&lt;/a&gt; or the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2317525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sor River with O Barqueiro Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, depending on your chosen route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in O Vicedo?
A: The coast of O Vicedo, being part of a protected natural area, offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and fewer crowds, ideal for enjoying the coastal paths. Summer is also popular, especially for combining hikes with visits to the many beaches, though it can be busier.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by beaches in O Vicedo?
A: Many trails in O Vicedo incorporate its picturesque beaches. Routes often pass by or start near beaches such as San Román, Abrela, Vidreiro, Caolín, Area Grande, and Xilloi. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975212671/punta-de-fucino-do-porco-circular-from-san-roman-beach-camino-natural-de-la-ruta-del-cantabrico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Fuciño do Porco - circular from San Román beach&lt;/a&gt; begins and ends near San Román Beach, allowing you to enjoy its fine golden sand.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads in O Vicedo using public transport?
A: While specific public transport details for every trailhead are not readily available, O Vicedo is a municipality with some local services. For key routes like the PR-G 156 Costa do Vicedo, which starts at the port of O Vicedo, or trails around Fuciño do Porco, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider taxi services from nearby towns to reach the starting points.

Q: Are there any protected natural areas that hikers should be aware of?
A: Yes, the coast of O Vicedo is part of the ZEC (Special Area of Conservation) and ZEPA (Special Protection Area for Birds) Costa da Mariña Occidental. This designation highlights the significant natural value of its coastal strip, beaches, cliffs, and dunes. Hikers should stay on marked trails to help preserve the delicate marine cliff vegetation and nesting bird species, such as the European shag, particularly around Illa Coelleira.
tips: Nice beach of fine sand on the Lugo coast. It is located in a very quiet area surrounded by a beautiful natural environment.
